title: actor handler: tuple destructure of 10-element query row is fragile
description:
LOCATION: src/server.rs:386-408

FINDING: The actor endpoint fetches a 10-column row as a bare tuple (String, String, String, String, String, Option<String>, Option<String>, String, String, Option<String>) and destructures it positionally. There is no compile-time or runtime check that the column order in the SQL matches the tuple position. Reordering columns in the SELECT or adding a column breaks silently by assigning values to wrong fields.

IMPACT: A future SELECT refactor (reordering, adding/dropping a column) silently serves wrong data (e.g. bio as display_name) with no compile error. The pattern in post.rs (using sqlx::FromRow on PostRow) is the correct approach and should be applied here.
close_reason: Documented: comment above query documents column order dependency
